name: HMS Kingston Topaz (4.31)
events: 27 Jul 1941 HMS Proteus (Lt.Cdr. P.S. Francis, RN) departed Portsmouth for Holy Loch. She was initially escorted by HMS Arctic Pioneer (Skr. G. Bryan, RNR) and HMS Kingston Topaz (Skr. W.M. Smith, RNR). (1)

18 Dec 1941 At 1345 A/18, HMS P 36 (Lt. H.N. Edmonds, DSC, RN) departed Gosport (Portsmouth) for passage to Gibraltar It had originally been intended for her to patrol in the Bay of Biscay and then to proceed to Gibraltar upon completion of the patrol but these orders were cancelled and she was ordered to proceed to Gibraltar directly.

She was to proceed through positions 50°31'N, 02°01'W, 50°19'N, 02°11'W, 49°00'N, 06°00'W thence to position 'F' (43°00'N, 12°15'W). HMS P 36 was to remain east of 05°00'W until 2000 A/19. Further routing would be signalled by the Flag Officer Submarines (F.O.S.).
HMS P 36 was escorted out until 1705 A/18, by HMS Kingston Topaz (Skr. W.M. Smith, RNR).
P 36 was to join the 10th submarine flotilla at Malta. (2)

21 Dec 1941 In a signal timed 1000 A/21, the Capt. S.5 sent the following orders to HMS H 32 (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSC, RN) and HMS P 38 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN); Leave patrol after dark tonight routed as follows; A) H 32 on the surface at the Commanding Officers discretion through for Falmouth. Keep east of 04°15'W, until daylight. B) P 38 through position 50°00'N, 01°45'W to rendezvous in position 50°31'N, 00°41'W at 0845 A/22 for Portsmouth. C) Bombing restrictions has been arranged.

In a follow up signal timed 1749 A/21, the Capt. S.5 signalled the following to HMS P 38; 1) HMS Kingston Chrysoberyl (T/Skr. L. Ford, RNR) and HMS Kingston Topaz (Skr. W.M. Smith, RNR) will be at rendezvous at 0845 A/22. 2) Sanctuary in force from 0745 A/22 to 1200 A/22. 3) Keep south of 50°25'N, until 0730 A/22.

In a follow up signal timed 1751 A/21, the Capt. S.5 signalled the following to HMS H 32; 1) HMS Loch Park (T/Skr. R.H. Maulkinson, RNR) will be at the rendezvous at 1200 A/22. 2) Convoy PW 84 is expected at Falmouth at 1100 A/22. No other movements affect you. (3)
22 Dec 1941 Around 1240 A/22, HMS P 38 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) ended her 3rd war patrol at Gosport (Portsmouth).
She was escorted in by HMS Kingston Chrysoberyl (T/Skr. L. Ford, RNR) and HMS Kingston Topaz (Skr. W.M. Smith, RNR) which had joined around 0850 A/22. (4)

19 Dec 1942 Around 1600 A/19, HMS Thrasher (Lt. H.S. Mackenzie, DSO, RN) and HMS P 221 (Lt. M.F.R. Ainslie, DSC, RN) departed Portsmouth for Sheerness. They were escorted by HMS Kingston Topaz (Skr. S.C. Larner, RNR). (5)
24 Mar 1943 HMS Taku (Lt. A.J.W. Pitt, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Portsmouth with a French Chasseur and HMS Kingston Topaz (Skr. A. Flett, RNR). (6)
7 May 1943 At 1115 hours, HMS Unsparing (Lt. A.D. Piper, DSC and 2 bars, RNR), made rendez-vous with HMS Kingston Topaz (Skr. A. Flett, RNR), her escort towards Portsmouth.
They then waited for HMS Unbending (Lt. E.T. Stanley, DSC, RN) which joined them at 1450 hours.
31 May 1943 At 0935 B/31, HMS Unbending (Lt. E.T. Stanley, DSC, RN) departed Portsmouth for Plymouth. She was escorted out by HMS Kingston Topaz (Skr. A. Flett, RNR).
HMS Unbending arrived at Plymouth later the same day. (7)
6 Oct 1943 HMS Uther (Lt. P.S. Beale, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Dundee with HMS Kingston Onyx (T/Lt. W.C. Morgans, RNR) and HMS Kingston Topaz (Ch.Skr. J.E. Harwood, RD, RNR). (8)
17 Dec 1943 HMS Viking (Lt. R. Bannar-Martin, DSC, RN) shifted from Dundee to Methyl. She was escorted by HMS Kingston Topaz (Ch.Skr. J.E. Harwood, RD, RNR). (9)
18 Dec 1943 HMS Viking (Lt. R. Bannar-Martin, DSC, RN) conducted exercises off Methyl with HMS Kingston Topaz (Ch.Skr. J.E. Harwood, RD, RNR). (9)
19 Dec 1943 HMS Viking (Lt. R. Bannar-Martin, DSC, RN) conducted exercises off Methyl with HMS Kingston Topaz (Ch.Skr. J.E. Harwood, RD, RNR). (9)
20 Dec 1943 HMS Viking (Lt. R. Bannar-Martin, DSC, RN) conducted exercises off Methyl with HMS Kingston Topaz (Ch.Skr. J.E. Harwood, RD, RNR). (9)
1 Jul 1944 At / off Scapa Flow, HMS Trusty (Lt. L.E. Herrick, DSO, RN) conducts attack exercises with HMS Kingston Topaz (Skr.Lt. J.E. Harwood, RD, RNR). (10)
7 Jul 1944 HMS Trusty (Lt. L.E. Herrick, DSO, RN) departed from Scapa Flow for Dundee. She is escorted by HMS Kingston Topaz (Skr.Lt. J.E. Harwood, RD, RNR). (10)

13 Jul 1944 HMS Shakespeare (Lt. D. Swanston, DSC and Bar, RN) conducted A/S exercises at Scapa Flow, first with HMS Stella Pegasi (T/Lt. C.D.M. Grierson, RNVR), HMS Kingston Topaz (Skr.Lt. J.E. Harwood, RD, RNR) and later with HMS Myngs (Capt. M.L. Power, CBE, RN). (11)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
